name: motor-rear-end-collision-with-credit-hire
userInput: I was stopped at traffic lights on the A406 last Tuesday and a van rear-ended me at maybe 25 mph. The other driver admitted fault at the scene and we exchanged details. My car is at the garage waiting for a repair quote and I've taken a hire car in the meantime. The other driver's insurer is now disputing the hire rate and saying I should have used basic hire. I want my car repaired, my hire car costs covered, and my excess back. I had a passenger but no one was injured.

caseData.issue: Rear-end collision at traffic lights; liability admitted at scene; insurer disputing credit-hire rate.
caseData.evidence: Photos of damage, dashcam footage of the collision, exchanged driver/insurer details, repair garage quote, hire vehicle agreement and rates.
caseData.loss: Vehicle repair (estimated £3,200), credit hire (£780 to date), recovery and storage (£180), policy excess (£500).
